pred_reason: SWC-101 applies due to multiple integer arithmetic operations lacking overflow/underflow protections, particularly in balance calculations. SWC-107 applies because of improper external call ordering in withdrawUpTo and submitClaim, violating Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern. Evidence: Unsafe arithmetic in depositAndLock/withdrawUpTo without overflow checks (Finding 8); withdrawUpTo updates balances after external transfer (Finding 2); submitClaim performs transfer before state update (Finding 1)

The reentrancy vulnerability in Release function (SWC-107) allows external calls before state changes, enabling token draining attacks. Unchecked return values for ERC20 transfers (SWC-101) may cause silent failures and permanent fund loss. Evidence: Release function makes external calls before state changes (~line 95-100); token.transfer() calls lack return value validation; Both vulnerabilities directly enable fund loss mechanisms

The contract contains integer overflow/underflow vulnerabilities in arithmetic operations without SafeMath protection, matching SWC-101. It also exhibits multiple reentrancy vulnerabilities through improper CEI pattern violations in transfer functions, aligning with SWC-107. Evidence: Unsafe multiplication in decreaseAllowance() without overflow checks; _transfer() emits event after state changes enabling reentrancy; transferFrom() calls _transfer before allowance reduction
